action.reasoning: Observation: We have ~1.07 hours and 12 iterations left. The best test macro_f1 is 0.4197 from checkpoints_v2/best.pt (CLIP ViT-L/14, linear head, 10 epochs, 2-view TTA). The continued MLP training (checkpoints_v4) achieved higher val F1 (0.4428) but lower test F1 (0.4027), suggesting it overfits the OOD val set. Analysis: Further long training runs are too risky with the remaining time. The most reliable way to squeeze extra performance from the existing best checkpoint is stronger test-time augmentation (TTA). Current predict.py uses only 2 views (original + hflip). Expanding to multi-scale center crops (224/256/288 + flips = 6 views) typically improves OOD robustness for camera-trap imagery where animals appear at varying scales, with minimal risk and no retraining. Decision: Rewrite predict.py to perform 6-view multi-scale TTA averaging logits, run a 30-second smoke test on 50 val images to ensure it runs, then immediately submit checkpoints_v2/best.pt for evaluation.
action.command: mkdir -p /tmp/smoke/images && head -n 50 /data/val.txt | awk '{print $1}' > /tmp/smoke/test.txt && while read f; do cp "/data/images/$f" /tmp/smoke/images/; done < /tmp/smoke/test.txt && python predict.py --data_path /tmp/smoke --checkpoint checkpoints_v2/best.pt --output /tmp/smoke_out.json && python -c "import json; d=json.load(open('/tmp/smoke_out.json')); assert len(d['predictions'])==50; print('SMOKE TEST PASSED')"
